subject: ap-world-history
title: Short Answer Question — AP World History
stimulus:
Election Poster for the German National Socialist Party, 1932
The German text in the poster reads, “Men! Women! Millions of men without work; millions of children without
futures. Save the German family—vote for Adolf Hitler.”
Album/ Alamy Stock Photo

parts.prompt: Identify ONE likely political purpose of the image.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The poster was created to inspire people to vote for the Nazi party.
1 pt: The poster was created to convince people that voting for the Nazi party would solve their economic problems.
1 pt: The poster was intended to make people angry with the current government and its management of the economy.

parts.prompt:
Explain ONE way the image illustrates the economic situation of the period after the First World
War.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The image shows the poverty that many families in Germany and elsewhere faced because of the Great Depression.
1 pt: The image illustrates the growing popularity of the idea that governments should take a more active role in economic life.
1 pt: The image shows that Germany suffered greatly following its defeat in the First World War, including unemployment, reparations, or inflation.

parts.prompt:
Explain ONE way the rise of the German National Socialist Party led to the Second World War.
3
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The rise of the Nazi Party contributed to aggressive militarism and the invasions of other countries.
1 pt: The rise of the Nazi Party led to intense nationalism and a desire to create “living space” for Germans through military expansion and conquest.

1 pt: After Hitler and the German National Socialist Party (the Nazis) gained totalitarian control over Germany, Hitler turned his attention to military domination of Europe, Africa, and other regions.
